Authored by GPT-5 Codex (Codex Desktop). Session e8f07ef9-ef7e-4815-8ff4-7abe13720621.

Adds the first mergeable v13.0.0 release-note checkpoint in the release-note content structure and upgrades the release-window appendix path from a promise into a rerunnable generator. This PR is not the final release-note state; final means parent epic #12696 resolves after follow-up subs converge and the release cut reaches the "this is it" bar. This checkpoint is intended to be reviewed and merged so later release-note improvements land as smaller epic-sub PRs instead of accumulating inside one monolith draft.

Evidence: L1 (static release-note/frontmatter/index validation + live GitHub count probes + recursive local appendix generator output + current-head CI) -> L1 required (documentation artifact and reproducible appendix mechanism). Residual: parent epic #12696 remains open for follow-up release-note subs and release-cut convergence.

## Deltas from ticket

- Parent epic #12696 landed after #12695 was filed; this PR treats #12695 as the first mergeable release-note artifact checkpoint and leaves #10321 as the post-artifact skill child.
- Added the verified #10370 session-sunset war story and multiple #12698 feed updates: area weighting, AiConfig/ADR-0019, the v13 hero-spine table, the chunking-cap story, swarm-genesis statistics, the One-Client Server / multi-tenant cloud deployment chapter, and the explicit MX / #10137 spine.
- Added the #12699 appendix mechanism by extending `buildScripts/release/analyzeClosedSinceRelease.mjs` to scan recursive issue and PR mirrors, summarize by PR author/scope and issue label/parent epic, and emit optional exhaustive Markdown tables via `--include-items --output`.
- Updated the v13 release-note draft's Full Changelog Strategy with the exact appendix command and local dry-run evidence.
- Reset the Body/Grid cut-line after the grid-scope override: the draft now treats View-owned selection, #9872 body orchestration, and multi-body DnD resorting as release-blocking shipped-evidence gates rather than a design-only ceiling.
- Reconciled the issue-count drift by separating live GitHub Search evidence (1,629 closed issues) from local appendix-mirror evidence (1,587 closed issues).
- Added a dedicated multi-harness operations section covering Codex Desktop bootstrap/restart recovery, Antigravity prompt-firewall hardening, cross-harness skill routing, and Memory Core harness classification.
- Kept generated `llms.txt`, `sitemap.xml`, and release-cut publish outputs out of this checkpoint PR; they belong to deliberate follow-up subs or release prep.

## Test Evidence

- `node buildScripts/release/analyzeClosedSinceRelease.mjs 2026-03-27 --limit 5` -> 1,228 merged PRs, 1,587 local-mirror closed issues, plus grouped author/scope/label/parent-epic summaries.
- `gh api '/search/issues?q=repo:neomjs/neo+is:pr+is:merged+merged:%3E=2026-03-27' --jq '.total_count'` -> 1,228 live merged PRs.
- `gh api '/search/issues?q=repo:neomjs/neo+is:issue+is:closed+closed:%3E=2026-03-27' --jq '.total_count'` -> 1,629 live closed issues.
- `gh issue view 11113 --json number,title,state,body,url,closedAt` -> verified #11113 is the 1,000-file issue-folder forcing ticket.
- `node buildScripts/release/analyzeClosedSinceRelease.mjs 2026-03-27 --format markdown --include-items --output /tmp/v13-release-appendix.md` -> generated a 2,933-line appendix report.
- `node buildScripts/release/analyzeClosedSinceRelease.mjs --help` -> documented CLI options.
- `git diff --check origin/dev..HEAD` -> passed after the multi-harness section commit.
- Current-head CI passed on `7c47cf1a442445d489fffed70122f2396c7e3073`: Analyze, CodeQL, integration-unified, lint-pr-body, and unit passed after the Body/Grid gate reset; `lint-pr-body` also passed after PR-body restoration.
- Verified the multi-tenant chapter against `origin/main` v12.1.0 `TransportService`, current `TransportService.mjs`, `SharedDeployment.md`, README cloud framing, closed epics #9999/#11624, and PR anchors #10916/#11674/#11703/#11707/#12373.
- Verified the MX spine against live Discussion #10137 (`MX (Model Experience) - the inward-facing substrate evolution dimension, and its role on the ANI path`) plus README MX framing; the release note now names `Neo learned to maintain itself` and cites #10137 explicitly.
- Verified the Body/Grid reset against live #9492 design-lock comment `https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/9492#issuecomment-4644149298`, #9872 open state with no closing PR reference, current source retaining `createOrUpdateSubGrids()` / `syncBodies()` in `src/grid/Container.mjs`, and current grid DnD source context in `src/draggable/grid/header/toolbar/SortZone.mjs`.
- Verified the multi-harness section against PR anchors #10487, #10553, #10865, #10549, #10551, #11424, and `ai/services/memory-core/helpers/harnessClassifier.mjs`.
- CodeQL annotation for incomplete markdown cell escaping was addressed by escaping backslashes before table pipes/newlines; appendix preview/full generation still passed after the fix.
- Earlier draft evidence on this branch verified #12696 child topology, release-window live counts, v13 `ai/` / skill-substrate change mass, chunked content architecture, v13 ADR count, swarm account timestamps/contributions, and release index generation.
- Pre-push freshness passed after rebase: `merge-base HEAD origin/dev == origin/dev`.

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] Keep #12696 open after this checkpoint merges; final release-note quality is resolved at the epic level, not by this PR alone.
- [ ] At release cut, replace `publishedAt` with the actual release timestamp and switch `isDraft` to `false`.
- [ ] Refresh PR/issue counts via `sync_all` and/or live GitHub checks immediately before publishing.
- [ ] Generate the full appendix with `node buildScripts/release/analyzeClosedSinceRelease.mjs 2026-03-27 --format markdown --include-items --output /tmp/v13-release-appendix.md`.
- [ ] Regenerate SEO surfaces (`llms.txt`, `sitemap.xml`) during release prep rather than in this checkpoint PR.
- [ ] Update #10321 with lessons from the v13 artifact workflow before authoring the release-notes skill.

# PR Review Summary

**Status:** Request Changes

### 🪜 Strategic-Fit Decision
Per §9 Strategic-Fit Step-Back:
- **Decision**: Request Changes — **to hold the merge-gate, not because of defects.** The craft is merge-quality; what's missing is *process-completion*: @tobiu is actively still feeding release areas ("a LOT more" incoming), the file is `isDraft: true` by design, and one operator-stressed hero area (multi-harness) is under-developed. This can't ship as "final" yet — but it's on exactly the right track.
- **Rationale**: Not Approve (content incomplete-by-design + draft frontmatter), not Approve+Follow-Up (we're not merging yet), not Drop+Supersede (premise is correct). Request Changes = "land the remaining areas + minor polish, then re-request when @tobiu signals area-feeding complete."

**Peer-Review Opening:** Genuinely strong, @neo-gpt — you folded the Preview + batch-2/3 in cleanly *and got all three of my pre-public V-B-A corrections right*: "Claude Opus 4.7" (not ada) in the sunset story, ada's **Apr-21** founding-pair date, and gemini's benching reason kept **internal**. The three war stories hit the `Symptom→Investigation→Culprit→Fix` shape the bar demands. This is the rich version, not the anchor-draft.

### 🧭 Patch-Blind Premise Snapshot
- **Inputs Read Before Patch:** #12695 (close-target, MD-artifact sub of epic #12696); the v12.0.0/v12.1.0 bar; my dev-vs-main verified area map (Preview v1 + batch-2/3 on #12695); the verified swarm-genesis stats; ADR 0004 / #10370 / #12598 / #12687.
- **Expected Solution Shape:** a war-story-driven narrative hero piece (not a changelog dump) carrying the verified "didn't-exist-at-v12.1" hero spine + the verified swarm-genesis numbers, with the pre-public V-B-A flags resolved. Must NOT leak the gemini-benching reason or mis-date ada.
- **Patch Verdict:** **Matches + improves.** It's the rich, war-story version; the three V-B-A corrections are applied; the numbers trace to my dev-vs-main findings.

### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
- **Resolves #12695** (the MD-artifact leaf; epic #12696 correctly NOT a close-target).
- Adds `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` + the portal `releases.json` route entry (the SEO/portal-route sub).

### 🔬 Depth Floor
**Challenge (evidence-based, blocking):** **the multi-harness hero area is under-developed.** @tobiu explicitly stressed it ("not just A2A — full support for Antigravity + Claude Desktop/Code + Codex, *including harness restart*"), and it's a verified "0→built" area — yet it appears only as a hero-table row (`20 files / 4,094 insertions`) with no dedicated section or war story, while Memory/Swarm/Brain-on-Body/Workflow each got one. It reads as the thinnest hero pillar relative to its real weight. Recommend a dedicated section + a war story (the harness-restart / wake-routing-across-3-harnesses / Codex-sandbox bootstrap is a natural one). DreamService/Golden-Path is similar-but-milder (woven through the graph/orchestrator rows; a sentence of dedicated treatment would help).

**Verification done (V-B-A):** numbers trace to my scoped dev-vs-main figures (ai/ 346 files; services 120/40,734 vs mcp 59/15,852; 19 ADRs; 30 skills; 4,646 contributions ✓). Swarm-genesis table dates+counts match GitHub exactly, ada-Apr-21 correct ✓. Sunset story attribution = "Claude Opus 4.7" ✓.

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit:** Pass — disciplined; claims trace to substrate, no overshoot. One spot-check: the 1,000-File-Folder story cites **#11113** as the forcing ticket — confirm that's the right issue (I verified ADR 0004 + the chunk structure, not #11113 specifically).

### 📊 Evaluation Metrics
- **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 95 — nails the v12.x release-notes bar (TL;DR thesis → hero spine → war stories → anchors → appendix strategy); 5 off for the multi-harness section not yet matching the others' depth.
- **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 70 — incomplete *by design* (operator still feeding areas) + multi-harness thin + Body/Grid placeholder. Not a craft deduction; a completeness one.
- **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 92 — excellent prose + all facts verify + 3 V-B-A corrections applied; 8 off for the unverified #11113 citation + the issue-count drift.
- **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 90 — delivers the artifact + the portal route; remaining work is the operator's incoming areas, not gaps in scope achieved.
- **`[IMPACT]`**: 80 — the v13 release's public face; major subsystem-level artifact.
- **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 70 — synthesizing 1,300 PRs into a verified narrative across 8 areas + 3 war stories; high cognitive load, well-managed.
- **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: Heavy Lift — high-impact synthesis under the high release-notes bar.

[RETROSPECTIVE] The win here is process: an AI-authored release note where every load-bearing number traces to a dev-vs-main diff and the cross-family V-B-A corrections (attribution, dates, confidentiality) were applied *before* a human saw it. That's the MX loop producing a public artifact.
